title: CODEX Sample Preparation and Staining Experiment Protocol
author: Kavya.Anjani

steps.title: Gather necessary materials Materials needed
steps.instruction: Gather necessary materials Materials needed: Coverslips (CODEX Mounting Kit 1.0 @RT) Poly-Lysine Solution 1% (CODEX Mounting Kit 1.0 @RT) 600 mL glass beaker Rubber band Saran wrap Milli-Q water Plastic petri dish Paper towels

steps.title: Coat coverslips with poly-lysine
steps.instruction: Coat coverslips with poly-lysine. *Incubating the coverslips in poly-lysine will need to take place least 12 hours prior to embedding tissue samples*

steps.title: Gently place 20 coverslips on the...
steps.instruction: Gently place 20 coverslips on the bottom of the glass beaker and slowly swirl the beaker to fan out the coverslips.

steps.title: Add 70 mL of poly-lysine solution...
steps.instruction: Add 70 mL of poly-lysine solution to the beaker, making sure to submerge all coverslips.

steps.title: To prevent evaporation, use the saran...
steps.instruction: To prevent evaporation, use the saran wrap to cover the beaker and seal the wrap with a rubber band.

steps.title: STOPPING POINT- Incubate coverslips in poly-lysine...
steps.instruction: STOPPING POINT- Incubate coverslips in poly-lysine solution for a minimum of 12:00:00 and up to one week at Room temperature .

steps.title: After incubation, dispose of the poly-lysine...
steps.instruction: After incubation, dispose of the poly-lysine solution into a proper waste container.

steps.title: Fill the beaker halfway with Milli-Q...
steps.instruction: Fill the beaker halfway with Milli-Q water and swirl contents to rinse coverslips.

steps.title: Let the beaker and coverslips sit for 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Let the beaker and coverslips sit for 00:01:00 .

steps.title: Dispose of the water into the sink
steps.instruction: Dispose of the water into the sink.

steps.title: Repeat steps 2
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 2.5-2.8 for a total of 7 washes.

steps.title: Fill the beaker halfway with Milli-Q water
steps.instruction: Fill the beaker halfway with Milli-Q water.

steps.title: Place two sets of paper towels on the benchtop
steps.instruction: Place two sets of paper towels on the benchtop.

steps.title: Remove the coverslips from the beaker,...
steps.instruction: Remove the coverslips from the beaker, then separate and lay each piece face down on the first set of paper towels.

steps.title: Invert each coverslip to dry the...
steps.instruction: Invert each coverslip to dry the reverse side on the second set of paper towels.

steps.title: Once the coverslips dry completely, collect...
steps.instruction: Once the coverslips dry completely, collect them in a plastic petri dish to store for up to 2 months.

steps.title: Cut and embed frozen tissues on poly-lysine coated coverslips
steps.instruction: Cut and embed frozen tissues on poly-lysine coated coverslips.

steps.title: Place poly-lysine coated coverslips in the cryostat chamber to equilibrate
steps.instruction: Place poly-lysine coated coverslips in the cryostat chamber to equilibrate.

steps.title: Cut frozen tissue slices with 5...
steps.instruction: Cut frozen tissue slices with 5 µm - 10 µm thickness. Do not exceed 10 µm

steps.title: Gently place tissue slice in the center of the coverslip
steps.instruction: Gently place tissue slice in the center of the coverslip.

steps.title: Place a gloved finger on the...
steps.instruction: Place a gloved finger on the underside of the coverslip behind the tissue slice to melt the OCT for better adherence. Do not exceed 2 seconds.

steps.title: Store tissue slices separated at -80...
steps.instruction: Store tissue slices separated at -80 °C for up to 6 months prior to staining.

steps.title: Gather necessary materials. Material needed
steps.instruction: Gather necessary materials. Material needed: Antibody Reduction Solution 2 (CODEX Conjugation Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Antibody Conjugation Solution (CODEX Conjugation Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Filter Blocking Solution (CODEX Conjugation Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Antibody Reduction Solution 1 (CODEX Conjugation Kit 1.0 @-20C, thaw right before use, one tube for every 3 antibodies) CODEX Antibody Tags (CODEX Conjugation Kit 1.0 @-20C, thaw right before use) Antibody Purification Solution (CODEX Conjugation Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Antibody Storage Solution (CODEX Conjugation Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Purified Antibodies (50 μg needed per antibody) 50 kDa MWCO filter 2 mL screw-top tubes Milli-Q water

steps.title: Calculate and obtain 50 µg of...
steps.instruction: Calculate and obtain 50 µg of each antibody that will be conjugated.

steps.title: Label a 50kDA MWCO filter unit...
steps.instruction: Label a 50kDA MWCO filter unit (both the filter device and the collection tube) for each antibody that will be conjugated.

steps.title: Add 500 µL of Filter Block...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL of Filter Block Solution to the filter device.

steps.title: Spin down the filter unit at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the filter unit at 12000 x g, °C, 2 minutes .

steps.title: Discard all liquid (from both the...
steps.instruction: Discard all liquid (from both the filter device and the collection tube).

steps.title: Add 50 µg of antibody to the filter device
steps.instruction: Add 50 µg of antibody to the filter device.

steps.title: Spin down the filter unit at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the filter unit at 12000 x g, °C, 8 minutes .

steps.title: Prepare the Antibody Reduction Master Mix while spinning
steps.instruction: Prepare the Antibody Reduction Master Mix while spinning. For one antibody, mix the following: 6.6 µL Antibody Reduction Solution 1 + 275 µL Antibody Reduction Solution 2 Multiply the solution 1 and solution 2 volumes by the total number of antibodies that will be conjugated to get the total volume needed from each reagent.

steps.title: Discard flow through
steps.instruction: Discard flow through.

steps.title: Add 260 µL of Antibody Reduction...
steps.instruction: Add 260 µL of Antibody Reduction Master Mix to each filter device.

steps.title: Vortex solution in filter device for 00:00:02 - 00:00:03
steps.instruction: Vortex solution in filter device for 00:00:02 - 00:00:03 .

steps.title: Incubate tube for 00:30:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate tube for 00:30:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Spin down the filter unit at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the filter unit at 12000 x g, °C, 8 minutes .

steps.title: Discard flow-through
steps.instruction: Discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add 450 µL of Antibody Conjugation...
steps.instruction: Add 450 µL of Antibody Conjugation Solution to each filter device.

steps.title: Spin down the filter unit at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the filter unit at 12000 x g, °C, 8 minutes .

steps.title: Prepare the CODEX antibody tag solution while spinning
steps.instruction: Prepare the CODEX antibody tag solution while spinning.

steps.title: Label a new tube for each...
steps.instruction: Label a new tube for each CODEX antibody tag (one tag per antibody).

steps.title: Add 210 µL of Antibody Conjugation...
steps.instruction: Add 210 µL of Antibody Conjugation Solution to each tube in step a. Set aside.

steps.title: Add 10 µL of Milli-Q water...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of Milli-Q water to resuspend each CODEX antibody tag (retrieved from -20 just prior). Mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Add the entire resuspended CODEX antibody...
steps.instruction: Add the entire resuspended CODEX antibody tag to step b and mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Discard flow-through
steps.instruction: Discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add the CODEX antibody tag solution...
steps.instruction: Add the CODEX antibody tag solution prepared in step 21 to the corresponding filter device.

steps.title: Close the lid and vortex the...
steps.instruction: Close the lid and vortex the filter unit for 2-3 sec.

steps.title: Incubate tube for 02:00:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate tube for 02:00:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: After 02:00:00 , set aside 5...
steps.instruction: After 02:00:00 , set aside 5 µL of the purified solution for QC.

steps.title: Spin down the filter unit at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the filter unit at 12000 x g, °C, 8 minutes .

steps.title: Discard flow-through
steps.instruction: Discard flow-through.

steps.title: Add 450 µL of Antibody Purification...
steps.instruction: Add 450 µL of Antibody Purification Solution to each filter device.

steps.title: Spin down the filter unit at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the filter unit at 12000 x g, °C, 8 minutes .

steps.title: Discard flow-through
steps.instruction: Discard flow-through.

steps.title: Repeat steps 29-31 two more times...
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 29-31 two more times for a total of three times.

steps.title: There will be solution left in the filter device
steps.instruction: There will be solution left in the filter device.

steps.title: Label a new collection tube for each filter device
steps.instruction: Label a new collection tube for each filter device.

steps.title: Add 100 µL Antibody Storage Solution to each filter device
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL Antibody Storage Solution to each filter device.

steps.title: Invert the filter device into its newly labeled collection tube
steps.instruction: Invert the filter device into its newly labeled collection tube.

steps.title: Spin down the filter unit at...
steps.instruction: Spin down the filter unit at 3000 x g, °C, 2 minutes .

steps.title: Transfer solution in the collection tube...
steps.instruction: Transfer solution in the collection tube to a labeled 2 mL screw-top tube and store at 4 °C for up to 1 year.

steps.title: Gather necessary materials. Materials needed
steps.instruction: Gather necessary materials. Materials needed: Hydration Buffer (CODEX Staining Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Staining Buffer (CODEX Staining Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Storage Buffer (CODEX Staining Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Blocking Component N (CODEX Staining Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Blocking Component J (CODEX Staining Kit 1.0 @ 4C) Blocking Component G (CODEX Staining Kit 1.0 @ -20C) Blocking Component S (CODEX Staining Kit 1.0 @ -20C) CODEX-Tagged Antibodies (CODEX Antibody Kit 1.0 @ -20C) Fixative (F) Aliquots (CODEX Staining Kit 1.0 @ -20C, thaw right before use, one tube for every 5 tissue samples) Acetone Methanol (Keep in -20C prior to starting experiment) 16% PFA 1 x DPBS 6-well TC plates Bent-tip tweezers Drierite Absorbents Eppendorf tubes Conical tubes 50 mL beakers Empty pipet tip boxes

steps.title: Prepare a humidity chamber
steps.instruction: Prepare a humidity chamber.

steps.title: Place wet paper towels at the...
steps.instruction: Place wet paper towels at the bottom of an empty pipet tip box.

steps.title: Fill the box with enough water...
steps.instruction: Fill the box with enough water to cover the towels completely.

steps.title: Recover the pipet tip tray and lid after rinsing
steps.instruction: Recover the pipet tip tray and lid after rinsing.

steps.title: Prepare Drierite absorbent beads
steps.instruction: Prepare Drierite absorbent beads.

steps.title: Fill an empty pipet tip box...
steps.instruction: Fill an empty pipet tip box with Drierite beads (about 1~2 cm deep).

steps.title: Prepare the following plate configuration for...
steps.instruction: Prepare the following plate configuration for CODEX solutions: *5 mL of each solution per well

steps.title: Prepare 10 mL of acetone in...
steps.instruction: Prepare 10 mL of acetone in a 50 mL beaker for each tissue.

steps.title: Remove the tissue sections from -80C...
steps.instruction: Remove the tissue sections from -80C and place the coverslips on Drierite absorbent beads.

steps.title: Let the tissue sections dry for 00:02:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Let the tissue sections dry for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Place the tissue samples face-up in the beakers containing acetone
steps.instruction: Place the tissue samples face-up in the beakers containing acetone.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:10:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:10:00 .

steps.title: Remove the coverslips from acetone and...
steps.instruction: Remove the coverslips from acetone and place them in the humidity chamber to dry for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Hydrate tissue samples following the plate configuration
steps.instruction: Hydrate tissue samples following the plate configuration.

steps.title: Place the coverslips in the Hydration...
steps.instruction: Place the coverslips in the Hydration Buffer wells (dip 2-3 times before fully submerging the slide). Ensure tissue is facing up.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Place the coverslips in the next...
steps.instruction: Place the coverslips in the next set of Hydration Buffer wells.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Prepare Pre-Staining Fixing Solution while incubating tissue
steps.instruction: Prepare Pre-Staining Fixing Solution while incubating tissue. For two samples, mix the following: 1 mL 16% PFA + 9 mL Hydration Buffer Multiply the PFA and buffer volumes by the total number of samples that will be stained to get the total volume needed from each reagent. Add 5 mL of Fixing Solution 1 to each well.

steps.title: Place the coverslips in the Pre-Staining Fixing Solution wells
steps.instruction: Place the coverslips in the Pre-Staining Fixing Solution wells.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Remove samples from Pre-Staining Fixing Solution...
steps.instruction: Remove samples from Pre-Staining Fixing Solution and rinse the samples by dunking the coverslips 2-3 times each in the top and bottom Hydration Buffer wells.

steps.title: Place the samples in the Staining...
steps.instruction: Place the samples in the Staining Buffer wells and let equilibrate for 00:20:00 - 00:30:00 .

steps.title: Prepare the antibody cocktail during equilibration
steps.instruction: Prepare the antibody cocktail during equilibration.

steps.title: To make CODEX Blocking Buffer for...
steps.instruction: To make CODEX Blocking Buffer for two samples, mix the following: 10 µL Blocking Component N + 10 µL Blocking Component G + 10 µL Blocking Component J + 10 µL Blocking Component S + 380 µL Staining Buffer Multiply the reagent volumes by the total number of samples that will be stained to get the total volume needed for the experiment.

steps.title: Calculate the total antibody volume per antibody cocktail
steps.instruction: Calculate the total antibody volume per antibody cocktail. Total Ab volume = (# of antibodies) x (# of samples) x (Ab volume/sample)] E.g. To stain two tissue samples with 24 antibodies and assuming 1 μL of antibody is needed per sample: Total Ab volume = 24 antibodies x 2 samples x 1 μL/sample = 48 μL

steps.title: Calculate to CODEX Blocking Buffer needed per antibody cocktail
steps.instruction: Calculate to CODEX Blocking Buffer needed per antibody cocktail. CODEX Blocking Buffer volume needed to make 210 μL Ab Cocktail Solution/sample = CODEX Blocking Buffer volume - Total Ab volume E.g. For two tissue samples with a total Ab volume of 48 μL: CODEX Blocking Buffer volume needed for Ab Cocktail Solution = 420 μL Blocking Buffer volume - 48 μL total Ab volume = 372 μL -> Ab Cocktail Solution = 48 μL Ab volume + 372 μL CODEX Blocking Buffer

steps.title: Combine the antibodies and the CODEX...
steps.instruction: Combine the antibodies and the CODEX Blocking Buffer to make 210 µL Antibody Cocktail Solution/tissue sample.

steps.title: Remove the coverslips from Staining Buffer...
steps.instruction: Remove the coverslips from Staining Buffer and place in the humidity chamber.

steps.title: Quickly add 200 µL of Antibody...
steps.instruction: Quickly add 200 µL of Antibody Cocktail Solution to each sample from the top corner of the coverslip.

steps.title: Place lid back on the humidity chamber
steps.instruction: Place lid back on the humidity chamber.

steps.title: Incubate for 03:00:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 03:00:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Prepare the following plate configuration for...
steps.instruction: Prepare the following plate configuration for CODEX solutions ~1 hour before the 3-hour incubation ends: *5 mL of each solution per well. Schematic is for two tissue samples.

steps.title: Wash the tissue samples following the plate configuration
steps.instruction: Wash the tissue samples following the plate configuration.

steps.title: Place the coverslips in the Staining...
steps.instruction: Place the coverslips in the Staining Buffer ( SB ) wells (dip 2-3 times before fully submerging the slide). Ensure tissue is facing up.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Place the coverslips in the next...
steps.instruction: Place the coverslips in the next set of Staining Buffer ( SB ) wells.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:02:00 .

steps.title: Prepare Post-Staining Fixing Solution (PS) while incubating tissue
steps.instruction: Prepare Post-Staining Fixing Solution (PS) while incubating tissue. For two samples, mix the following: 1 mL 16% PFA + 9 mL Storage Buffer Multiply the PFA and buffer volumes by the total number of samples that will be stained to get the total volume needed from each reagent. Add 5 mL of Post-Staining Fixing Solution to each well.

steps.title: Place the coverslips in the Post-Staining Fixing Solution (PS) wells
steps.instruction: Place the coverslips in the Post-Staining Fixing Solution (PS) wells.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .

steps.title: Prepare ice-cold methanol during incubation
steps.instruction: Prepare ice-cold methanol during incubation. Place a 6-well TC plate on ice. Pipet methanol up and down to equilibrate the serological pipet tip. Add 5 mL methanol to one well per sample.

steps.title: Remove samples from Post-Staining Fixing Solution...
steps.instruction: Remove samples from Post-Staining Fixing Solution ( PS ) and rinse the samples by dunking the coverslips 2-3 times each in the 1x DPBS ( PBS ) wells for a total of 3 washes.

steps.title: Remove the coverslips from 1x DPBS...
steps.instruction: Remove the coverslips from 1x DPBS ( PBS ) and place in ice-cold methanol.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 .

steps.title: Bring plate containing 1x DPBS (...
steps.instruction: Bring plate containing 1x DPBS ( PBS ) to the ice bucket for quick transfer from methanol.

steps.title: After incubation, rinse the samples by...
steps.instruction: After incubation, rinse the samples by dunking the coverslips 2-3 times each in the 1x DPBS ( PBS ) wells for a total of 3 washes.

steps.title: Rinse the humidity chamber if not previously washed
steps.instruction: Rinse the humidity chamber if not previously washed.

steps.title: Prepare the Final Fixative Solution
steps.instruction: Prepare the Final Fixative Solution. For 1-5 samples, mix the following: 1000 µL 1x DPBS + 20 µL CODEX Fixative Reagent *20 μL of CODEX Fixative Reagent is one aliquot, thaw and spin down right before use. Vortex gently to mix.

steps.title: Remove coverslips from wells and place...
steps.instruction: Remove coverslips from wells and place them in the humidity chamber.

steps.title: Add Room temperature of Final Fixative...
steps.instruction: Add Room temperature of Final Fixative Solution to the top corner of each sample.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:20:00
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:20:00 .

steps.title: Remove samples from the humidity chamber...
steps.instruction: Remove samples from the humidity chamber and rinse the samples by dunking the coverslips 2-3 times each in the 1x DPBS ( PBS ) wells for a total of 3 washes.

steps.title: Place the coverslips in a new...
steps.instruction: Place the coverslips in a new TC plate with 5 mL Storage Buffer (SB) per sample in each well.

steps.title: STOPPING POINT- use samples directly to...
steps.instruction: STOPPING POINT- use samples directly to run a CODEX experiment or store up to two weeks at 4C.

steps.title: Gather necessary materials
steps.instruction: Gather necessary materials: Materials needed: 96-well plates (CODEX Mounting Kit 1.0 @ RT) 96-well plate foil seal (CODEX Mounting Kit 1.0 @ RT) 10X CODEX Buffer (CODEX Assay Kit 1.0 @ RT) CODEX Assay Reagent (CODEX Assay Kit 1.0 @ -20C) CODEX Nuclear Stain (CODEX Assay Kit 1.0 @ -20C) CODEX-Tagged Dyes (CODEX Antibody Kit 1.0 and CODEX Conjugation Kit 1.0 @ -20C) Nuclease-free water 1.5 mL amber Eppendorf tubes

steps.title: Determine the CODEX-tagged dyes combination for each running cycle
steps.instruction: Determine the CODEX-tagged dyes combination for each running cycle. Each cycle may contain up to one HOECHST/DAPI dye, one FAM/FITC dye, one Cy3/TRITC dye, and one Cy5 dye.

steps.title: Label an amber Eppendorf tube for each CODEX-tagged dyes combination
steps.instruction: Label an amber Eppendorf tube for each CODEX-tagged dyes combination.

steps.title: Prepare the stock solution of CODEX Reporter Stock Solution
steps.instruction: Prepare the stock solution of CODEX Reporter Stock Solution. For 5 wells (cycles) per plate, mix the following: 150 µL 10x CODEX Buffer + 125 µL Assay Reagent + 5 µL Nuclear Stain + 1220 µL Nuclease-free water Double or triple the volume of each reagent if running 10 or 15 cycles per plate. Mix solution by gently inverting.

steps.title: Add CODEX Reporter Stock Solution to...
steps.instruction: Add CODEX Reporter Stock Solution to each labeled amber Eppendorf tube. A B C D 1 3 CODEX-tagged dyes 2 CODEX-tagged dyes 1 CODEX-tagged dye 2 CODEX Reporter Stock Solution Volume per tube 235 μL 240 μL 245 μL

steps.title: Thaw each CODEX-tagged dye immediately before use
steps.instruction: Thaw each CODEX-tagged dye immediately before use.

steps.title: Quickly spin down the CODEX-tagged dyes
steps.instruction: Quickly spin down the CODEX-tagged dyes.

steps.title: Add 5 µL of each CODEX-tagged...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of each CODEX-tagged dye to its corresponding tube.

steps.title: Mix contents of each tube by gently pipetting
steps.instruction: Mix contents of each tube by gently pipetting.

steps.title: Transfer 245 µL of solution from...
steps.instruction: Transfer 245 µL of solution from each tube to its designated well in the 96-well plate.

steps.title: Prepare a strip of foil seal...
steps.instruction: Prepare a strip of foil seal and cover all wells containing CODEX-tagged dyes.

steps.title: STOPPING POINT- use dyes directly to...
steps.instruction: STOPPING POINT- use dyes directly to run a CODEX experiment or store up to two weeks at 4C.

steps.title: Gather necessary materials. Materials needed
steps.instruction: Gather necessary materials. Materials needed: 96-well plate foil seal (CODEX Mounting Kit 1.0 @ RT) 2 CODEX Gaskets (CODEX Mounting Kit 1.0 @ RT) 10X CODEX Buffer (CODEX Assay Kit 1.0 @ RT) CODEX Nuclear Stain (CODEX Assay Kit 1.0 @ -20C) Stage Insert with Lid (CODEX Instrument) 600-2000 mL glass beakers Bent-tip tweezers 1.5 mL and 2 mL Eppendorf tubes Milli-Q water DMSO Aspirator setup Pipette tip box lid Ice Bucket

steps.title: Turn on the CODEX Instrument
steps.instruction: Turn on the CODEX Instrument.

steps.title: Remove the antibody-stained tissue section(s) adhered...
steps.instruction: Remove the antibody-stained tissue section(s) adhered to the Poly-lysine coated coverslip and the sealed, pre-loaded 96-well plate containing the Reporter Master Mix solutions from 4C. Allow them to equilibrate to Room temperature for at least 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Prepare 1X CODEX Assay Buffers in 600-2000 mL glass beakers
steps.instruction: Prepare 1X CODEX Assay Buffers in 600-2000 mL glass beakers. A B C D E F G H I 1 5 CODEX cycles 8 CODEX cycles 10 CODEX cycles 12 CODEX cycles 15 CODEX cycles 18 CODEX cycles 20 CODEX cycles 25 CODEX cycles 2 10X CODEX Buffer 31 mL 44 mL 53.3 mL 63.3 mL 78.5 mL 93.5 mL 103.5 mL 128.5 mL 3 Milli-Q water 281 mL 394 mL 480.5 mL 570.5 mL 705.5 mL 840.5 mL 930.5 mL 1155.5 mL 4 Total 312 mL 438 mL 534 mL 634 mL 784 mL 934 mL 1034 mL 1284 mL 5 DMSO 236 mL 318 mL 386 mL 454 mL 556 mL 658 mL 726 mL 896 mL 1X CODEX Buffer Set aside 7 mL of 1x CODEX Buffer in a 15 mL conical tube for later use.

steps.title: Place generated 1x CODEX Buffer in...
steps.instruction: Place generated 1x CODEX Buffer in the CODEX Buffer Bottle, labeled Bottle One and DMSO in the amber bottle, labeled Bottle Two.

steps.title: Load the stage insert
steps.instruction: Load the stage insert.

steps.title: Untwist the knobs at the top...
steps.instruction: Untwist the knobs at the top of the stage insert to remove the lid with the fluidic lines.

steps.title: Remove the old coverslip using the...
steps.instruction: Remove the old coverslip using the forceps, push the corners of the gaskets towards the center to release the seal between the coverslip and the gasket.

steps.title: Gently remove the coverslip using the forceps
steps.instruction: Gently remove the coverslip using the forceps.

steps.title: If the coverslip breaks, remove all...
steps.instruction: If the coverslip breaks, remove all glass pieces and rinse with water.

steps.title: Soak fresh gaskets in 1X CODEX...
steps.instruction: Soak fresh gaskets in 1X CODEX Buffer or in Storage Buffer for a few seconds.

steps.title: Place the first gasket inside the...
steps.instruction: Place the first gasket inside the squared well at the center of the stage insert.

steps.title: Gently tap with forceps to ensure...
steps.instruction: Gently tap with forceps to ensure it adheres to the stage insert surface.

steps.title: Gently place the tissue sample on...
steps.instruction: Gently place the tissue sample on top of the gasket, making sure it is perfectly inserted and the tissue is facing upwards.

steps.title: Quickly and gently tap the coverslip...
steps.instruction: Quickly and gently tap the coverslip edges with the forceps to ensure it adheres to the gasket.

steps.title: Quickly place the second gasket on...
steps.instruction: Quickly place the second gasket on top of the sample coverslip.

steps.title: Quickly place the lid on top of the stage insert
steps.instruction: Quickly place the lid on top of the stage insert.

steps.title: Secure the lid by turning the knobs
steps.instruction: Secure the lid by turning the knobs.

steps.title: Dispense 700 µL of 1X CODEX...
steps.instruction: Dispense 700 µL of 1X CODEX Buffer on the corner of the sample.

steps.title: Remove any salts formed on the...
steps.instruction: Remove any salts formed on the coverslip from drying using a kimwipe with Milli-Q water. Use a dry kimwipe to remove residual liquid.

steps.title: Stain the CODEX sample with HOECHST
steps.instruction: Stain the CODEX sample with HOECHST.

steps.title: Thaw CODEX Nuclear Staining Solution
steps.instruction: Thaw CODEX Nuclear Staining Solution.

steps.title: Have the 7 mL of 1x...
steps.instruction: Have the 7 mL of 1x CODEX Buffer set aside previously within reach.

steps.title: Prepare a 2 mL Eppendorf tube...
steps.instruction: Prepare a 2 mL Eppendorf tube for the Nuclear Stain Solution. Prepare the Nuclear Stain Solution. 1999 µL 1x CODEX Buffer + 1 µL Nuclear Staining Solution

steps.title: Remove the solution within the sample well
steps.instruction: Remove the solution within the sample well.

steps.title: Add 700 µL of Nuclear Stain Solution to the sample
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL of Nuclear Stain Solution to the sample.

steps.title: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature . Cover it from light.

steps.title: Remove the solution within the sample well
steps.instruction: Remove the solution within the sample well.

steps.title: Add 700 μL of 1x CODEX Buffer
steps.instruction: Add 700 μL of 1x CODEX Buffer.

steps.title: Repeat steps 83
steps.instruction: Repeat steps 83.7-83.8 for a total of 5 washes.

steps.title: Add 700 µL of 1x CODEX Buffer
steps.instruction: Add 700 µL of 1x CODEX Buffer.

steps.title: Launch the CODEX Instrument Management Software...
steps.instruction: Launch the CODEX Instrument Management Software and enter cycle, marker id and exposure time information. Minimize the window.

steps.title: Place the plate with the CODEX-tagged...
steps.instruction: Place the plate with the CODEX-tagged dyes in its designated place in the instrument.

steps.title: Clean any dried buffer on the...
steps.instruction: Clean any dried buffer on the bottom of the coverslip with a wet kimwipe and dry off any residual liquid.

steps.title: Turn on the Keyence scanner and...
steps.instruction: Turn on the Keyence scanner and load the cassette into the scanner.
